item.title: Feds reveal new details of alleged plot to attack White House UFC event
item.description: The FBI said it disrupted an attempt to attack Sunday's UFC America 250 event at the White House, with court records detailing an alleged plot to use small drones carrying explosives.

item.title: What to know as Kevin Warsh prepares for his first Federal Reserve meeting
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh is set to lead his first meeting and announce what's next for the nation's interest rate. CBS News' Kelly O'Grady reports.

item.title: U.S. citizen released, no charges after accusations of abuse in Colombia
item.description: A video of the alleged incident circulated widely on social media, but Colombia's president later said the man "apparently did not" abuse any children.

item.title: Iran's desire to keep Hezbollah alive could derail U.S. deal, expert warns
item.description: Iran's links to the Hezbollah militant group, which continues to fight Israel in Lebanon, could derail the U.S. deal on the table for President Trump. CBS News' Aaron MacLean joins with more insight.

item.title: Dead or stranded: Commercial sailors suffer in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: India alone has more than 18,000 sailors stuck across the Persian Gulf region. Some tell CBS News it feels like they're "in jail."

item.title: These were the plans for the alleged White House-UFC attack, according to an affidavit
item.description: The FBI on Tuesday said it thwarted an attempt to attack Sunday's UFC America 250 event at the White House. Court records show what the alleged plot entailed. CBS News' Jake Rosen has those details and former NYPD Deputy Commissioner Richard Esposito joins with analysis.

item.title: Rival Trump, Kemp endorsements could sway Georgia's Republican Senate runoff election
item.description: Today's Republican runoff in Georgia will determine who faces Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff in November. President Trump and Gov. Brian Kemp's opposing endorsements highlight a split in the Republican Party, CBS News political director Fin Gómez explains.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: SNOO baby bassinets maker issued warning by FDA
item.description: The FDA sent a warning letter to Happiest Baby Inc., alleging the company sold some unauthorized products and cited unsanitary conditions.

item.title: Rabbi helps Jamaicans impacted by devastating Hurricane Melissa
item.description: Jamaica was ravaged by Hurricane Melissa late in October 2025. Months later, efforts to rebuild across the island continue. Chabad of Jamaica's Rabbi Yaakov Raskin reflects on the ongoing recovery process.

item.title: Flooding slams Texas and prompts disaster declaration
item.description: Flooding in Texas prompted Gov. Greg Abbott to issue a disaster declaration on Monday. CBS News' Jason Allen has the latest.

item.title: Russian artist and Putin critic shot dead in Poland
item.description: Semyon Skrepetsky was known for performances and artwork attacking Russian President Vladimir Putin​ and other authoritarian leaders.

item.title: Israel must pull troops out of Lebanon under deal with U.S., Iran says
item.description: Iran's foreign minister says Israeli troops can't remain in Lebanon under the pending deal with the U.S.

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns says he's "just grateful" after Knicks win NBA title
item.description: The New York Knicks starting center Karl-Anthony Towns speaks to "CBS Mornings" about his team winning the NBA championship.

item.title: 3 debt issues to address before you retire and claim Social Security
item.description: If you're closing in on retirement, there are a few debt problems you should deal with before making other moves.

item.title: What are today's HELOC and home equity loan interest rates?
item.description: HELOCs and home equity loans offer homeowners an affordable way to borrow money now. Here are the rates for each.

item.title: Despite Trump's assurances, shippers wait and see on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump says there's a "safe, secure and pristine" route through the Strait of Hormuz, but major shipping companies aren't convinced.

item.title: Trump, Kemp choose opposing candidates in Georgia Republican Senate runoff election
item.description: Voters across multiple states will cast their ballots today to decide local and state elections. Georgia voters will decide which candidates continue to the general election in several key runoff contests. CBS News' Fin Gómez has more context.

item.title: World awaits U.S.-Iran nuclear negotiations as some Strait of Hormuz traffic resumes
item.description: The U.S. and Iran are set to officially sign a framework agreement on Friday, as the world awaits the outcome of the nuclear negotiations expected to follow. CBS News' Nancy Cordes and Will Todman, a senior fellow at the Center for Strategic and International Studies, have more.

item.title: Alleged plot targeting White House UFC event involved explosive drones, documents show
item.description: The alleged plot targeting the White House's UFC event on Sunday involved drones and explosives, court documents show. At least one suspect, 19-year-old Tycen Proper, has been arrested in Ohio. CBS News' Jake Rosen has more.

item.title: How Gavin Newsom's claims about a DOJ investigation could impact a potential 2028 presidential run
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom said that the Justice Department is investigating him and his wife in a video released Monday. CBS News California's Julie Watts breaks down how it might impact a potential 2028 presidential run for the popular Democrat.

item.title: SpaceX to buy AI coding assistant Cursor for $60 billion
item.description: The deal comes just days after SpaceX went public in the largest IPO in history, raising $75 billion to help fund its expansion.

item.title: Georgia and Alabama GOP runoffs and more primaries to watch today
item.description: Voters are casting their ballots in four states and the District of Columbia on Tuesday, including the runoff race in Georgia to take on Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff.

item.title: Georgia voters head to polls for primary runoffs including GOP Senate, governor
item.description: Voters in Georgia are headed to the polls Tuesday for key elections, including a GOP Senate primary runoff between Trump-endorsed Rep. Mike Collins and former football coach Derek Dooley. CBS News' Skyler Henry has more.

item.title: Trump says he thinks Iran negotiations will "go pretty quickly"
item.description: President Trump said that he thinks negotiations with Iran will "go pretty quickly" at the G7 Summit after both countries signed a memorandum of understanding remotely, with an official signing set for Friday. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi and Imtiaz Tyab have more.

item.title: Yum! Brands sells struggling Pizza Hut in $2.7 billion deal
item.description: The sale will split ownership of the pizza chain between a U.S.-based private equity firm and a Chinese restaurant company.

item.title: Being a sports fan is good for you and the country, expert says
item.description: The collective joy at a big sports win can actually improve your overall well-being, social scientist and CBS News contributor Arthur Brooks explains. Sports fandom "gives you a sense of belonging, and that's really important," Brooks says.

item.title: "Star Wars" lightsaber and more iconic film props going up for auction
item.description: Luke Skywalker's lightsaber from the "Star Wars" sequel "The Empire Strikes Back" is expected to sell for at least $1 million at an upcoming auction.

item.title: 3 CD account questions to ask after this week's Fed meeting
item.description: The Federal Reserve will meet again this week. Here are three CD account questions savers should ask afterwards.

item.title: Ohio voting rights group facing criminal fraud investigation, sources say
item.description: The FBI executed a search warrant at the office of an Ohio-based group known for its voter registration work as part of an ongoing fraud-related investigation, sources told CBS News.
